steps.title: Set the equations equal to form a quadratic
Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content:
For $Ax^2+Bx+C=0$, no real solutions means the discriminant is negative. The boundary case is discriminant $0$:
$$10^2-4(1)(a+0.5)=0$$
Desmos can solve that boundary directly and gives $a=24.5$. To have no real solutions, $a$ must be greater than $24.5$.

steps.title: Solve the inequality for a
steps.content:
$$100 - 4a - 2 \lt 0$$
$$98 - 4a \lt 0$$
$$4a \gt 98$$
$$a \gt 24.5$$
Since $$a$$ is a positive integer, the least possible value is $$a = 25$$.
The answer is 25.
